fields.description:
# ACLI subprocess 429 handling: bounded backoff, honor Retry-After if available

## Context
All ACLI calls go through `_run_acli` (`acli_subprocess.py`), which retries all non-zero exit codes (except
401/assignee) with a uniform 2s/4s sleep. `_call_with_retry` (`dispatch_one.py:100-144`) adds 429/5xx
classification and already raises the EXISTING `RetryExhaustedError` (`_errors.py`). `_call_with_backoff`
(`acli_subprocess.py:236-292`) is dead code (no production call sites; it wraps `urllib`, NOT the subprocess
`CalledProcessError` domain). ACLI is a subprocess, so Jira `Retry-After` availability is UNVERIFIED.

## What (minimal, contract-preserving â€” a plan, not an implementation)
1. PROBE FIRST: against a rate-limited/staged endpoint, record (a) whether `Retry-After` is parseable in stderr,
   (b) the exit code ACLI returns on HTTP 429, (c) whether ACLI retries 429 internally. Record on the ticket.
2. Add 429-aware jittered exponential backoff with a BOUNDED max-retry/total ceiling to the LIVE `_run_acli` retry
   loop (so continuous 429s cannot hang the subprocess). Honor `Retry-After` IFF the probe confirmed it; else
   backoff without it. If ACLI retries 429 internally, no Python-layer change for 429 (documented).
   CONTRACT-PRESERVING: the ceiling only BOUNDS attempts â€” on exhaustion `_run_acli` raises exactly what it raises
   today (`CalledProcessError`). Do NOT introduce or surface a new exception type from `_run_acli`; the 9 call
   sites' contract is unchanged. (`RetryExhaustedError` stays where it is, in the app-layer `_call_with_retry`.)
3. Retryable-code scope: ADD-ON (recommended) â€” the 429 branch REPLACES the uniform sleep for that attempt (no
   double-sleep); all other exit codes keep their current retry behavior (zero caller impact). NARROW to
   {429,502,503} would break other non-zero codes and is OUT OF SCOPE unless separately decided.
4. `_call_with_backoff` (dead) is OPTIONAL cleanup only, NOT required by this ticket. If a follow-up removes it,
   that follow-up also drops its `acli.py:57` import, its `__all__` entry, and the `test_error_taxonomy.py`
   reference â€” but this ticket leaves it untouched.
5. PRESERVE the non-idempotent-write guard (`AcliTimeoutError` is not a `TimeoutError`).
6. Observability: log rate-limit detection, backoff trigger, Retry-After honored-vs-unavailable, ceiling reached.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Probe recorded: Retry-After availability, the 429 exit code, whether ACLI retries 429 internally (+ the Python outcome for the internal case).
- [ ] Bounded jittered exp-backoff added to the live `_run_acli` loop; honors Retry-After IFF probe-confirmed; add-on mode does not double-sleep; on exhaustion the EXISTING `CalledProcessError` contract is unchanged (no new exception surfaced to the 9 callers).
- [ ] Other non-zero exit codes retain current retry behavior (ADD-ON); NARROW is explicitly out of scope.
- [ ] No-blind-retry-on-timeout guard preserved; new backoff covered by a case in `tests/.../test_acli_subprocess_timeout.py`.
- [ ] Rate-limit/backoff/Retry-After/ceiling events logged; the retry policy is captured in an ADR/design note.
